name: Model Guidelines — OpenAI o3-mini
description: Reasoning-focused instructions for using OpenAI o3-mini on complex or ambiguous engineering tasks.

sections.title: OpenAI o3-mini — When To Use
sections.content:
## OpenAI o3-mini — When To Use

Use **o3-mini** for tasks that benefit from deeper reasoning or multi-step planning, for example:

- Non-trivial refactors across multiple modules or layers.
- Designing or migrating architectures (APIs, services, data models).
- Debugging issues that require **hypothesis generation and refinement**.
- Analysing performance issues, race conditions, or complex state flows.

For straightforward edits (small bug fixes, copy changes, simple components), prefer the **default coding model** instead.

sections.title: OpenAI o3-mini — Working Style
sections.content:
## OpenAI o3-mini — Working Style

### Reasoning Expectations

- Produce a **short, structured plan** before making large or cross-cutting changes.
- Keep internal reasoning **concise and focused on code**; avoid lengthy general essays.
- Explicitly call out **assumptions** and where they may be wrong.

### Code Changes

- Even when a plan is complex, implement it as a **series of small, reviewable steps**.
- After major changes, include a brief **self-review** describing what was changed and how to verify it.
- Prefer **maintainability and clarity** over micro-optimisations unless performance is the primary goal.

### Hand-off To Simpler Models

- When detailed reasoning is complete, summarise the approach so that a simpler coding model (e.g. gpt-4o) can execute follow-up tasks without re-deriving the plan.
